title: Links Embedded in Form fields
excerpt: I have encountered this issue for months, but just now it became worse in the sense that the links are no longer working. Whenever pasting a link into a field in the forms, Monday.com has been automatically including wor…

posts.author: syoussef (Sarah Y)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have encountered this issue for months, but just now it became worse in the sense that the links are no longer working. Whenever pasting a link into a field in the forms, Monday.com has been automatically including words before/after the link as part of the hyperlink. Today, I’ve started to encounter the links no longer working because the word is actually being included in the link.

If I copy and paste the hyperlink from the text below, this is what I get: Page not found,

(“FRI,” should not be included!)

Screenshot 2025-01-27 at 2.45.39 PM635×109 11.6 KB

posts.author: SirEric (Eric Maranga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @syoussef ,

Could you provide a bit more context? Is this issue occurring with a Monday.com form or an external form? Additionally, where is the data being outputted—directly into a Monday board or somewhere else?

I’ve tested a Monday.com form with a link field, and it seems to work as expected when the data is displayed on Monday boards. The links appear correctly and function without any issues.

image1627×422 37.8 KB

If the issue persists, as a workaround, I’d recommend using a URL shortener like Bitly to minimize the chances of extra characters being appended to the hyperlink. This can help ensure the links remain clean and functional.

Let me know if you need further assistance!

posts.author: shayan.moussawi (Shayan Moussawi)
posts.cookedHtml:
This doesn’t look like a monday issue:

Are you copying the link from MS Teams?

This is a known Teams issue where it will also copy the name of the person you are chatting to

posts.author: syoussef (Sarah Y)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi thanks for responding. It’s a Monday.com form (not a board) and it’s a link to an external website. Also, I have tried bitly and tiny url to shorten but no luck… copying and pasting a link often ends up somehow connecting a word both visibly when I preview the form and in the hyperlink when I click on it.

So, in summary, if I paste a link into the text box at the top of the form or a question in the form, then I preview the form, clicking on the link doesn’t direct me to the external site I’m trying to reach.

Thank again!

posts.author: syoussef (Sarah Y)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, as stated I am creating a Monday.com form. No connection whatsoever with Teams.

posts.author: Ag47Panda (Steve Wasson)
posts.cookedHtml: I have seen similar. If you are going into a text field try switching it to a link field.

posts.author: syoussef (Sarah Y)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Steve, how do you switch to a link field?

posts.author: MarigoldsInMay (Darielle Shandler)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @syoussef - check out the information in this post I found - it turns out you can use html coding to add a hyper link into the form description. I was having the same issue you were having and this is a great fix!

Hyperlinks on Forms Feature requests
Hey Nachliel from Easyapps here You can add hyperlinks to a form description as well as column descriptions by utilizing there HTML support. This is done by adding an “a” tag to the form description in the format of <a href="https://www.easyappshq.com/">Open Link</a> Regardless, if you are interested in creating styled forms that better match your brand and embed perfectly in you web page you can check out our monday.com app Easyform and our extensive documentation site. Additionally, not l…

posts.author: MarigoldsInMay (Darielle Shandler)
posts.cookedHtml: You’re welcome! So happy to have found the answer!
